candidates.title: Yield-normalized design of biomass porous carbon for aqueous supercapacitors
candidates.science_question: Can activation benefits be evaluated by a yield-normalized, electrode-relevant metric rather than gravimetric capacitance alone?
candidates.hypothesis: Moderate activation that preserves yield and electrode density can outperform extreme activation under practical metrics.
candidates.ai_role: optional: literature meta-analysis and candidate prioritization, not primary novelty

candidates.title: Composition-normalized screening of biomass hard-carbon precursors
candidates.science_question: Do cellulose/lignin/ash descriptors explain SIB hard-carbon ICE better than biomass labels?
candidates.hypothesis: Composition-normalized descriptors reduce label noise and improve early-cycle performance interpretation.
candidates.ai_role: useful: descriptor ranking and small-data modeling after frozen experimental protocol

candidates.title: Ion-accessible pore descriptors for aqueous supercapacitor biomass carbons
candidates.science_question: Which pore descriptors remain predictive under realistic mass loading and electrode thickness?
candidates.hypothesis: Ion-accessible pore volume and electrode engineering descriptors outperform BET alone for practical SC performance.
candidates.ai_role: useful: multi-descriptor correlation and uncertainty-aware screening
